sentences.sentence: In today's hypercompetitive marketplace, companies that successfully introduce new products are more likely to flourish than those that don't.
sentences.translation: 在当今竞争极其激烈的市场中，成功推出新产品的公司比那些没有成功推出的公司更有可能蓬勃发展。
difficult_points: that successfully introduce new products 是修饰 companies 的定语从句。
than those that don't 中 don't 后省略了 introduce new products，指未成功推出新产品的公司。

sentences.sentence: businesses spend billions of dollars making better "mousetraps" only to find consumers roundly rejecting them.
sentences.translation: 企业投入数十亿美元去打造更好的“捕鼠器”，却发现消费者纷纷拒绝购买。

structure.text: only to find consumers roundly rejecting them

difficult_points: 句首 businesses 首字母小写，是原文的排印错误，正常应为句首大写 Businesses，此处按原文保留。
making better "mousetraps" 是现在分词短语，说明企业花钱要做的事情。
only to find 表示“结果却发现”，暗示事与愿违的转折。
"mousetraps" 在此比喻创新产品，源自“建造更好的捕鼠器”这一商业谚语。

sentences.sentence: Studies show that new products fail at the stunning rate of between 40% and 90%, depending on the category, and the odds haven't changed much in the past 25 years.
sentences.translation: 研究表明，新产品的失败率惊人地高达40%到90%（具体取决于品类），而且这一比例在过去25年里几乎没有变化。

structure.text: that new products fail at the stunning rate of between 40% and 90%
and the odds haven't changed much in the past 25 years

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，作 show 的宾语。
depending on the category 是分词短语作状语，说明失败率因产品类别而异。
the odds 指代前文提到的40%到90%这一失败率情况。

sentences.sentence: In the U.S. packaged goods industry, for instance, companies introduce 30,000 products every year, but 70% to 90% of them don't stay on store shelves for more than 12 months.
sentences.translation: 例如，在美国包装消费品行业，企业每年推出3万种新产品，但其中70%到90%在货架上存续不超过12个月。

structure.text: In the U.S. packaged goods industry, for instance
but 70% to 90% of them don't stay on store shelves for more than 12 months

difficult_points: for instance 是插入语，引出具体例子。
them 指代前文的 30,000 products。
don't stay on ... for more than 12 months 表示产品在货架上存续不超过12个月即被下架。

sentences.sentence: Most innovative products – those that create new product categories or revolutionize old ones – are also unsuccessful.
sentences.translation: 大多数创新产品——即那些开创新产品类别或彻底革新旧产品类别的产品——同样以失败告终。

structure.text: those that create new product categories or revolutionize old ones
that create new product categories or revolutionize old ones

difficult_points: 破折号之间的内容是对 Most innovative products 的同位语解释。
ones 代替 product categories，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: According to one study,47% of first movers have failed, meaning that approximately half the companies that pioneered new product categories later pulled out of those businesses.
sentences.translation: 根据一项研究，47%的先行者最终以失败告终，也就是说，大约有一半率先开创新产品类别的公司后来退出了这些业务。

structure.text: meaning that approximately half the companies
later pulled out of those businesses

difficult_points: study,47% 之间原文缺少空格，属排印错误，此处按原文保留。
meaning that ... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，补充说明前述数据的含义。
that pioneered new product categories 是修饰 companies 的定语从句。

expressions.meaning_zh: 先行者；率先进入市场的企业

sentences.sentence: After the fact, experts and novices alike tend to dismiss unsuccessful innovations as bad ideas that were destined to fail.
sentences.translation: 事后，无论是专家还是外行，都倾向于把失败的创新贬低为注定失败的坏点子。

structure.text: tend to dismiss unsuccessful innovations as bad ideas

difficult_points: experts and novices alike 意为“专家和外行都一样”，alike 修饰前面并列的两个名词。
dismiss A as B 表示“把A当作B而不予理会/否定”。
that were destined to fail 修饰 bad ideas，意为“注定要失败的”。

sentences.sentence: Why do consumers fail to buy innovative products even when they offer distinct improvements over existing ones?
sentences.translation: 为什么即使创新产品相对现有产品有明显改进，消费者仍然不愿购买呢？

structure.text: do consumers fail to buy innovative products
even when they offer distinct improvements over existing ones

difficult_points: even when 引导让步/时间状语从句，强调即使有明显改进这一事实。
they 指代前文的 innovative products；ones 代替 products，指现有产品。

sentences.sentence: Why do companies invariably have more faith in new products than is warranted?
sentences.translation: 为什么企业对新产品的信心总是超出应有的程度？

structure.text: do companies invariably have more faith in new products

difficult_points: than is warranted 是比较状语从句，省略了主语，完整形式接近 than [the faith that] is warranted，意为“超过合理应有的程度”。
invariably 表示“总是、一贯如此”，强调这种过度自信具有普遍性。

sentences.sentence: Few would question the objective advantages of many innovations over existing alternatives, but that's often not enough for them to succeed.
sentences.translation: 很少有人会质疑许多创新相对现有替代品的客观优势，但这通常还不足以让它们获得成功。

structure.text: Few would question the objective advantages of many innovations over existing alternatives
but that's often not enough for them to succeed

difficult_points: Few 作主语表示“很少有人”，含部分否定意味。
that 指代前面提到的“客观优势的存在”这一事实。
for them to succeed 中 for them 是不定式的逻辑主语，them 指 innovations。

sentences.sentence: To understand why new products fail to live up to companies' expectations, we must delve into the psychology of behavior change.
sentences.translation: 要理解新产品为何未能达到企业的预期，我们必须深入研究行为改变背后的心理学。

structure.text: To understand why new products fail to live up to companies' expectations
why new products fail to live up to companies' expectations
we must delve into the psychology of behavior change

difficult_points: 句首不定式短语作目的状语，why 引导的从句作 understand 的宾语。
live up to 表示“达到（期望、标准）”。

sentences.sentence: New products often require consumers to change their behavior.
sentences.translation: 新产品往往要求消费者改变自己的行为。

structure.text: often require consumers to change their behavior

difficult_points: require sb to do sth 表示“要求某人做某事”。
their 指代 consumers。

sentences.sentence: As companies know, those behavior changes entail costs.
sentences.translation: 正如企业所知，这些行为改变会带来成本。
difficult_points: As 引导方式状语从句，意为“正如……”。
entail 意为“伴随产生、招致”，比 cause 更强调必然性。

sentences.sentence: Consumers costs, such as the activation fees they have to pay when they switch from one cellular service provider to another.
sentences.translation: 消费者需要承担成本，比如从一家移动服务提供商转到另一家时必须支付的开通费。

structure.text: when they switch from one cellular service provider to another

difficult_points: 原文此句缺少谓语动词，Consumers costs 疑为排印错误，推测原意接近 Consumers bear costs（消费者承担成本），此处按原文保留。
activation fees 后省略了关系代词 that/which，they have to pay 是省略关系词的定语从句。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明产生开通费的具体情境。

sentences.sentence: They also bear learning costs, such as when they shift from manual to automatic automobile transmissions.
sentences.translation: 他们还要承担学习成本，比如从手动挡汽车换成自动挡汽车时需要重新学习操作。

structure.text: such as when they shift from manual to automatic automobile transmissions

difficult_points: bear costs 表示“承担成本”。
such as when ... 引出具体情境作举例说明。

sentences.sentence: People sustain obsolescence costs, too.
sentences.translation: 人们还要承受因产品过时而产生的成本。
difficult_points: sustain 在此意为“承受、遭受”，与 bear 同义。
obsolescence costs 指因旧设备/技能过时而产生的损失成本。

sentences.sentence: For example, when they switch from VCRs to DVD players, their videotape collections become useless.
sentences.translation: 例如，当人们从录像机换成DVD播放器时，他们收藏的录像带就变得毫无用处了。

structure.text: when they switch from VCRs to DVD players

difficult_points: when 引导时间状语从句，说明产生过时成本的具体场景。
their 指代前文的 they（消费者）。

sentences.sentence: All of these are economic switching costs that most companies routinely anticipate.
sentences.translation: 所有这些都是大多数企业惯常会预见到的经济性转换成本。
difficult_points: these 指代前文提到的 activation fees、learning costs、obsolescence costs 等各类成本。
that 引导定语从句修饰 economic switching costs。

sentences.sentence: What businesses don't take into account, however, are the psychological costs associated with behavior change.
sentences.translation: 然而，企业没有考虑到的是与行为改变相关的心理成本。

structure.text: What businesses don't take into account

difficult_points: What businesses don't take into account 是 what 引导的主语从句。
associated with behavior change 是过去分词短语，修饰 psychological costs。

sentences.sentence: Many products fail because of a universal, but largely ignored, psychological bias: People irrationally overvalue benefits they currently possess relative to those they don't.The bias leads consumers to value the advantages of products they own more than the benefits of new ones.
sentences.translation: 许多产品的失败源于一种普遍存在却常被忽视的心理偏差：人们会不理性地高估自己目前拥有的利益，而相对贬低那些自己没有的利益。这种偏差使消费者更看重自己已拥有产品的优点，而非新产品的优点。

structure.text: because of a universal, but largely ignored, psychological bias
to value the advantages of products they own more than the benefits of new ones

difficult_points: 原文在 don't 后缺少空格，The bias 与前一句直接粘连，属排印错误；实际上这里是两句独立的句子，本条按原文合并保留。
benefits 后省略了关系代词 that/which，they currently possess 是省略关系词的定语从句。
those they don't 中 don't 后省略了 possess，指人们没有拥有的那些利益。
products they own 中省略了关系代词，they own 修饰 products。

sentences.sentence: It also leads executives to value the benefits of innovations they've developed over the advantages of incumbent products.
sentences.translation: 这种偏差还使高管们更看重自己所开发的创新产品的优点，而非现有产品的优势。

structure.text: also leads executives to value the benefits of innovations
over the advantages of incumbent products

difficult_points: It 指代前一句提到的 the bias（心理偏差）。
innovations 后省略关系代词，they've developed 修饰 innovations。
value A over B 表示“看重A胜过B”。

sentences.sentence: Companies have long assumed that people will adopt new products that deliver more value or utility than existing ones.
sentences.translation: 企业长期以来一直认为，人们会采用那些比现有产品带来更多价值或效用的新产品。

structure.text: that people will adopt new products
that deliver more value or utility than existing ones

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导宾语从句作 assumed 的宾语。
第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 new products。
ones 代替 products，指现有产品。

sentences.sentence: Thus, businesses need only to develop innovations that are objectively superior to incumbent products, and consumers will have sufficient incentive to purchase them.
sentences.translation: 因此，企业只需要开发出客观上优于现有产品的创新产品，消费者就会有足够的动力去购买它们。

structure.text: businesses need only to develop innovations
that are objectively superior to incumbent products
and consumers will have sufficient incentive to purchase them

difficult_points: need only to do 强调“只需要做……即可”。
that are objectively superior to incumbent products 是修饰 innovations 的定语从句。
them 指代 innovations。

sentences.sentence: In the 1960s, communications scholar Everett Rogers called the concept"relative advantage" and identified it as the most critical driver of new-product adoption.
sentences.translation: 20世纪60年代，传播学学者埃弗雷特·罗杰斯将这一概念称为“相对优势”，并将其视为新产品被采用的最关键驱动因素。

structure.text: and identified it as the most critical driver of new-product adoption

difficult_points: 原文 concept 与引号之间缺少空格，属排印错误，此处按原文保留。
called ... 与 identified ... 是两个并列谓语，共用主语 Everett Rogers。
it 指代前文的 the concept（即“相对优势”）。

sentences.sentence: This argument assumes that companies make unbiased assessments of innovations and of consumers, likelihood of adopting them.
sentences.translation: 这一论点假定，企业能够对创新产品以及消费者采用这些产品的可能性做出不带偏见的评估。

structure.text: that companies make unbiased assessments of innovations and of consumers, likelihood of adopting them

difficult_points: 原文 consumers, likelihood 之间应为 consumers' likelihood（消费者的可能性），此处缺少所有格撇号且多了一个逗号，属排印错误，按原文保留。
assessments of A and of B 中 A、B 并列，均为 assessments 的对象。
them 指代 innovations。

sentences.sentence: Although compelling, the theory has one major flaw: It fails to capture the psychological biases that affect decision making.
sentences.translation: 尽管这一理论颇具说服力，却存在一个重大缺陷：它未能涵盖影响决策的心理偏差。

structure.text: the theory has one major flaw
It fails to capture the psychological biases

difficult_points: Although compelling 是让步状语从句的省略形式，补全为 Although it is compelling。
冒号后的内容具体解释 one major flaw 是什么。
that affect decision making 是修饰 psychological biases 的定语从句。

sentences.sentence: In 2002, psychologist Daniel Kahneman won the Nobel Prize in economics for a body of work that explores why and when individuals deviate from rational economic behavior.
sentences.translation: 2002年，心理学家丹尼尔·卡尼曼因一系列研究成果获得了诺贝尔经济学奖，这些研究探讨了个体在何时、为何会偏离理性经济行为。

structure.text: won the Nobel Prize in economics
that explores why and when individuals deviate from rational economic behavior

difficult_points: for a body of work 说明获奖原因，for 表示“因为”。
that 引导定语从句修饰 a body of work。
why and when 并列引导两个宾语从句，作 explores 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: One of the cornerstones of that research, developed with psychologist Amos Tversky, is how individuals value prospects, or choices, in the marketplace.
sentences.translation: 该研究的基石之一——与心理学家阿莫斯·特沃斯基共同完成——是关于个体如何评估市场中的前景，也就是各种选择。

structure.text: One of the cornerstones of that research
is how individuals value prospects, or choices, in the marketplace
how individuals value prospects, or choices, in the marketplace

difficult_points: developed with psychologist Amos Tversky 是插入的过去分词短语，补充说明研究的合作背景。
how 引导表语从句，说明这块基石具体研究了什么。
prospects, or choices 中 or 表示同位解释，即 prospects 就是 choices。

sentences.sentence: Kahneman and Tversky showed, and others have confirmed, that human beings' responses to the alternatives before they have four distinct characteristics.
sentences.translation: 卡尼曼和特沃斯基的研究表明——后来也被其他学者证实——人类面对眼前各种选择时的反应具有四个明显特征。

structure.text: that human beings' responses to the alternatives before they have four distinct characteristics

difficult_points: and others have confirmed 是插入语，补充说明该结论后来也被证实。
that 引导的从句同时作 showed 和 confirmed 的宾语。
before they 中 they 疑似应为 them（指代 alternatives，即摆在人们面前的选择），此处按原文保留。

sentences.sentence: First, people evaluate the attractiveness of an alternative based not on its objective, or actual, value but on its subjective, or perceived value.
sentences.translation: 第一，人们评估一个选择的吸引力时，依据的不是其客观（即实际）价值，而是其主观（即感知）价值。

structure.text: people evaluate the attractiveness of an alternative
based not on its objective, or actual, value but on its subjective, or perceived value

difficult_points: not A but B 结构强调依据的是 B（主观价值）而非 A（客观价值）。
or actual 和 or perceived 分别是对 objective 和 subjective 的同位解释。

sentences.sentence: Second, consumers evaluate new products or investments relative to a reference point, usually the products they already own or consume.
sentences.translation: 第二，消费者评估新产品或投资时，会参照一个基准点，通常就是他们已经拥有或正在使用的产品。

structure.text: consumers evaluate new products or investments
usually the products they already own or consume

difficult_points: relative to 表示“相对于、参照”。
usually the products they already own or consume 是对 a reference point 的同位补充说明，中间省略了关系代词 that/which。

sentences.sentence: Third, people view any improvements relative to this reference point as gains and treat all shortcomings as losses.
sentences.translation: 第三，人们会把相对于这个基准点的任何改进都视为收益，而把所有不足都当作损失。

structure.text: people view any improvements relative to this reference point as gains
and treat all shortcomings as losses

difficult_points: view A as B 与 treat A as B 是并列谓语，共用主语 people。
relative to this reference point 修饰 improvements，说明改进的参照对象。

sentences.sentence: Fourth, and most important, losses have a far greater impact on people than similarly sized gains, a phenomenon that Kahneman and Tversky called "loss aversion."For instance, studies show that most people will not accept a bet in which there is a 50% chance of winning $100 and a 50% chance of losing $100.
sentences.translation: 第四，也是最重要的一点：损失给人们带来的影响远大于同等规模的收益，卡尼曼和特沃斯基把这种现象称为“损失厌恶”。例如，研究表明，大多数人不会接受这样一个赌局——有50%的概率赢得100美元，也有50%的概率输掉100美元。

structure.text: losses have a far greater impact on people than similarly sized gains
a phenomenon that Kahneman and Tversky called "loss aversion."
that Kahneman and Tversky called "loss aversion."
that most people will not accept a bet
in which there is a 50% chance of winning $100 and a 50% chance of losing $100

difficult_points: 原文在 aversion. 后缺少空格，For instance 与前一句直接粘连，属排印错误；实际上这是两句独立的句子，本条按原文合并保留。
a phenomenon that ... 是对前一分句内容的同位概括。
have a greater impact on ... than ... 是比较结构，突出损失影响大于收益。
in which 引导定语从句修饰 a bet，说明赌局的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: The gains from the wager must outweigh the losses by a factor of between two and three before most people find such a bet attractive.
sentences.translation: 赌局带来的收益必须是损失的两到三倍，大多数人才会觉得这样的赌局具有吸引力。

structure.text: by a factor of between two and three
before most people find such a bet attractive

difficult_points: outweigh ... by a factor of X 表示“超过……X倍”。
before 在此并非单纯表示时间上的“之前”，而是表示“达到某种程度才……”的条件性关系。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, a survey of 1,500 customers of Pacific Gas and Electric revealed that consumers demand three to four times more compensation to endure a power outage – and suffer a loss – than they are willing to pay to avoid the problem, a potential gain.
sentences.translation: 同样，一项针对太平洋煤气电力公司1500名客户的调查显示，消费者若要忍受停电（即承受一次损失），要求获得的补偿是他们为避免停电（即获得一次潜在收益）而愿意支付金额的三到四倍。

structure.text: a survey of 1,500 customers of Pacific Gas and Electric
that consumers demand three to four times more compensation to endure a power outage
than they are willing to pay to avoid the problem

difficult_points: and suffer a loss 是插入语，补充说明忍受停电即意味着承受一次损失。
demand X more compensation than ... 是比较结构，强调补偿要求是支付意愿的三到四倍。
a potential gain 是对 avoid the problem 这一行为的同位概括，指其带来的潜在收益。

sentences.sentence: As Kahneman and Tversky wrote, "losses loom larger than gains."
sentences.translation: 正如卡尼曼和特沃斯基所写的那样，“损失比收益更为显眼”。
difficult_points: As 引导方式状语从句，引出卡尼曼和特沃斯基的原话。
loom larger than 意为“显得比……更突出、更严重”。

sentences.sentence: Loss aversion leads people to value products that they already possess – those that are part of their endowment – more than those they don't have.
sentences.translation: 损失厌恶使人们更看重自己已经拥有的产品——即属于其禀赋的那部分——而非那些自己没有的产品。

structure.text: those that are part of their endowment
more than those they don't have

difficult_points: products 后省略关系代词，that they already possess 是定语从句。
破折号中的 those that are part of their endowment 是对 products that they already possess 的同位解释。
those they don't have 中省略了关系代词，指人们没有的产品。

sentences.sentence: According to behavioral economist Richard Thaler, consumers value what they own, but many have to give up, much more than they value what they don't own but could obtain.
sentences.translation: 行为经济学家理查德·塞勒指出，相较于自己不曾拥有但可以获得的东西，消费者更看重自己拥有、但可能不得不放弃的东西。

structure.text: According to behavioral economist Richard Thaler
but many have to give up
much more than they value what they don't own but could obtain

difficult_points: what they own 和 what they don't own but could obtain 分别是 value 的宾语从句，构成对比。
but many have to give up 是插入语，补充说明这些拥有的东西中有许多可能不得不放弃。
much more than 加强比较的程度，强调差距之大。

sentences.sentence: Thaler called that bias the "endowment effect."
sentences.translation: 塞勒把这种偏差称为“禀赋效应”。
difficult_points: call A B 表示“把A称为B”。
that bias 指代前文所述的、人们更看重已拥有物品的心理偏差。

sentences.sentence: In a 1990 paper, Thaler and his colleagues describe a series of experiments they conducted to measure the magnitude of the endowment effect.
sentences.translation: 在一篇1990年发表的论文中，塞勒和同事们描述了一系列他们为测量禀赋效应大小而设计的实验。

structure.text: to measure the magnitude of the endowment effect

difficult_points: experiments 后省略关系代词，they conducted 是定语从句。
to measure the magnitude of the endowment effect 是不定式短语作目的状语。

sentences.sentence: In one such experiment, they gave coffee mugs to a group of people, the Sellers, and asked at what price point – from 25 cents to $9.25– the Sellers would be willing to part with those mugs.
sentences.translation: 在其中一个实验中，他们把咖啡杯发给一组人（称为“卖家”），并询问在从25美分到9.25美元之间的哪个价位上，卖家愿意放弃这些杯子。

structure.text: they gave coffee mugs to a group of people
the Sellers would be willing to part with those mugs

difficult_points: the Sellers 是对 a group of people 的同位说明，即把这组人称为“卖家”。
at what price point ... the Sellers would be willing to part with those mugs 是 asked 的宾语从句，属于疑问词引导的间接问句。
from 25 cents to $9.25 是插入语，说明价位的范围。

sentences.sentence: They asked another group – the Choosers – to whom they didn't give coffee mugs, to indicate whether they would choose the mug or the money at each price point.
sentences.translation: 他们又询问了另一组人（称为“选择者”，没有得到咖啡杯），请这组人在每个价位上指出自己会选择杯子还是选择等值的钱。

structure.text: to whom they didn't give coffee mugs
to indicate whether they would choose the mug or the money at each price point
whether they would choose the mug or the money at each price point

difficult_points: the Choosers 是对 another group 的同位说明。
to whom they didn't give coffee mugs 是修饰 another group 的定语从句，whom 指代这组人。
whether ... or ... 引导宾语从句，作 indicate 的宾语，表示“选择A还是B”。

sentences.sentence: In objective terms, all the Sellers and Choosers were in the same situation: They were choosing between a mug and a sum of money.
sentences.translation: 客观而言，所有卖家和选择者所面临的情况其实是一样的：他们都是在一个杯子和一笔钱之间做选择。

structure.text: all the Sellers and Choosers were in the same situation
They were choosing between a mug and a sum of money

difficult_points: 冒号后的内容具体解释了 the same situation 指的是什么。
choose between A and B 表示“在A和B之间做选择”。

sentences.sentence: In one trial of this experiment, the Sellers priced the mug at $7.12, on average, but the Choosers were willing to pay only $3.12.
sentences.translation: 在这个实验的一次试验中，卖家给杯子的平均定价是7.12美元，而选择者却只愿意支付3.12美元。

structure.text: In one trial of this experiment
the Sellers priced the mug at $7.12, on average
but the Choosers were willing to pay only $3.12

difficult_points: on average 是插入语，说明7.12美元是平均定价。
but 连接两个对比鲜明的并列分句，突出卖家与选择者估值的巨大差异。

sentences.sentence: In another trial, the Sellers and the Choosers valued the mug at $7.00 and $3.50, respectively.
sentences.translation: 在另一次试验中，卖家和选择者对杯子的估值分别是7美元和3.5美元。

structure.text: the Sellers and the Choosers valued the mug at $7.00 and $3.50

difficult_points: respectively 表示前后两组数值分别对应前面并列的两个主语（the Sellers、the Choosers）。
another trial 表明这是与前一句不同的又一次独立试验，用于印证结果的稳定性。

sentences.sentence: Overall, the Sellers always demanded at least twice as much to give up the mugs as the Choosers would pay to obtain them.
sentences.translation: 总体而言，卖家为放弃杯子所要求的金额，始终至少是选择者为获得杯子而愿意支付金额的两倍。

structure.text: the Sellers always demanded at least twice as much
as the Choosers would pay to obtain them

difficult_points: twice as much ... as ... 是倍数比较结构，表示“是……的两倍”。
them 指代 the mugs。

sentences.sentence: Kahneman and Tversky's research also explains why people tend to stick with what they have even if a better alternative exists.
sentences.translation: 卡尼曼和特沃斯基的研究还解释了为什么人们即使存在更好的选择，也倾向于坚持自己已有的东西。

structure.text: why people tend to stick with what they have
even if a better alternative exists

difficult_points: why 引导宾语从句，作 explains 的宾语。
what they have 是介词 with 的宾语从句，指人们已经拥有的东西。
even if 引导让步状语从句。

sentences.sentence: In a 1989 paper, economist Jack Knetsch provided a compelling demonstration of what economists William Samuelson and Richard Zeckhauser called the "status quo bias."
sentences.translation: 在一篇1989年发表的论文中，经济学家杰克·内奇有力地展示了经济学家威廉·萨缪尔森和理查德·泽克豪泽所称的“现状偏见”。

structure.text: what economists William Samuelson and Richard Zeckhauser called the "status quo bias."

difficult_points: what 引导的从句作介词 of 的宾语，从句内部是 call A B 结构（把A称为B）。
"status quo bias" 是萨缪尔森和泽克豪泽提出的概念名称。

sentences.sentence: Knetsch asked one group of students to choose between an attractive coffee mug and a large bar of Swiss chocolate.
sentences.translation: 内奇请一组学生在一个精美的咖啡杯和一大块瑞士巧克力之间做选择。

structure.text: asked one group of students to choose between an attractive coffee mug and a large bar of Swiss chocolate

difficult_points: ask sb to do sth 表示“请/要求某人做某事”。
choose between A and B 表示在两者之间选择。

sentences.sentence: He gave a second group of students the coffee mugs but a short time later allowed each student to exchange his or her mug for a chocolate bar.
sentences.translation: 他给第二组学生发了咖啡杯，但不久后又允许每个学生把自己的杯子换成一块巧克力。

structure.text: gave a second group of students the coffee mugs
but a short time later allowed each student to exchange his or her mug for a chocolate bar

difficult_points: give sb sth 是双宾语结构。
exchange A for B 表示“用A换取B”。

sentences.sentence: Finally, Knetsch gave chocolate bars to a third group of students but much later allowed each student to exchange his or her bar for a mug.
sentences.translation: 最后，内奇给第三组学生发了巧克力，但过了更长时间后，才允许每个学生把巧克力换成杯子。

structure.text: Knetsch gave chocolate bars to a third group of students
but much later allowed each student to exchange his or her bar for a mug

difficult_points: much later 与前一句的 a short time later 形成对比，强调等待时间更长。
exchange his or her bar for a mug 结构与前句呼应，方向相反（巧克力换杯子）。

sentences.sentence: Of the students given a choice at the outset, 56% chose the mug, and 44% chose the chocolate bar, indicating a near even split in preferences between the two products.
sentences.translation: 在一开始就被给予自由选择权的学生中，56%选择了杯子，44%选择了巧克力，这表明两种产品之间的偏好几乎是对半分的。

structure.text: Of the students given a choice at the outset
given a choice at the outset
indicating a near even split in preferences between the two products

difficult_points: given a choice at the outset 是过去分词短语，修饰 the students，指他们一开始就可以自由选择。
indicating ... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，总结前述数据说明的现象。

sentences.sentence: Logically, therefore, about half of the students to whom Knetsch gave the coffee mug should have traded for the chocolate bar and vice versa.
sentences.translation: 因此，从逻辑上讲，内奇给了咖啡杯的那组学生中应该有大约一半人会把杯子换成巧克力，反之亦然。

structure.text: to whom Knetsch gave the coffee mug
should have traded for the chocolate bar

difficult_points: to whom Knetsch gave the coffee mug 是修饰 the students 的定语从句。
should have traded 表示“按逻辑推理本应发生却未发生”的虚拟语气。
vice versa 表示“反之亦然”，即巧克力组也应有一半换成杯子。

sentences.sentence: That didn't happen.
sentences.translation: 但这种情况并没有发生。
difficult_points: That 指代前一句提到的“大约一半学生会换取另一种物品”这一预期情况。
这句话简短有力，用于承接上文的逻辑推测并直接给出否定结果，形成语气上的转折。
didn't happen 使用一般过去时的否定形式，强调实际情况与逻辑推测相反。

sentences.sentence: Only 11% of the students who had been given the mugs and 10% of those who had been given the chocolate bars wanted to exchange their products.
sentences.translation: 在拿到杯子的学生中，只有11%想换成巧克力；在拿到巧克力的学生中，也只有10%想换成杯子。

structure.text: who had been given the mugs
who had been given the chocolate bars

difficult_points: who had been given the mugs / who had been given the chocolate bars 分别是修饰 the students 和 those 的定语从句，用过去完成时被动语态。
those 代替 the students，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: To approximately 90% of the students, giving up what they already had seemed like a painful loss and shrank their desire to trade.
sentences.translation: 对大约90%的学生而言，放弃自己已经拥有的东西感觉像是一种痛苦的损失，这削弱了他们交换的意愿。

structure.text: giving up what they already had
and shrank their desire to trade

difficult_points: giving up what they already had 是动名词短语作主语，what they already had 是 giving up 的宾语从句。
seemed like 和 shrank 是并列谓语，共用同一个动名词主语。

sentences.sentence: Interestingly, most people seem oblivious to the existence of the behaviors implicit in the endowment effect and the status quo bias.
sentences.translation: 有趣的是，大多数人似乎完全没有意识到禀赋效应和现状偏见中所隐含的这些行为倾向的存在。

structure.text: seem oblivious to the existence of the behaviors
implicit in the endowment effect and the status quo bias

difficult_points: be oblivious to 表示“对……毫无察觉、浑然不知”。
implicit in ... 修饰 the behaviors，说明这些行为倾向内在于禀赋效应和现状偏见之中。

sentences.sentence: In study after study, when researchers presented people with evidence that they had irrationally overvalued the status quo, they were shocked, skeptical, and more than a bit defensive.
sentences.translation: 在一项又一项研究中，当研究者向人们展示证据、表明他们不理性地高估了现状时，人们的反应是震惊、怀疑，甚至颇有些抵触情绪。

structure.text: when researchers presented people with evidence
that they had irrationally overvalued the status quo
they were shocked, skeptical, and more than a bit defensive

difficult_points: study after study 表示“一项又一项研究”，强调普遍性。
present sb with sth 表示“向某人展示某物”。
that 引导同位语从句，说明 evidence 的具体内容。

expressions.meaning_zh: 向某人出示/展示某事物

sentences.sentence: These behavioral tendencies are universal, but awareness of them is not.
sentences.translation: 这些行为倾向普遍存在，但人们对它们的意识却并非如此。

structure.text: but awareness of them is not

difficult_points: is not 后面省略了 universal，完整应为 is not universal，即人们对这些倾向的自我意识并不普遍。
them 指代 these behavioral tendencies。
